function: Convert rider hand input into compatible caliper actuation
potential_failure_mode: Incorrect lever mode or lever-caliper pairing produces unsuitable cable pull
potential_effect: Excessive or insufficient braking force with poor controllability
potential_cause: Mode switch set for wrong brake type or incompatible lever selected
recommended_action: Add lever-caliper compatibility checklist and mode-position verification step
critic_notes: Specific lever compatibility mechanism grounded in manual; keep

function: Provide reachable handbrake control from normal riding position
potential_failure_mode: Lever reach or location prevents timely full handbrake actuation
potential_effect: Delayed braking response and longer stopping distance
potential_cause: Poor lever placement inappropriate reach adjustment or handlebar fit
recommended_action: Define lever reach acceptance check for intended rider size range
critic_notes: Lower severity than loss of brake path; keep

function: Transmit force without interfering with rotating rotor
potential_failure_mode: Protruding inner cable end contacts the disc brake rotor
potential_effect: Wheel lock and forward fall risk
potential_cause: Cable end left longer than specified or not secured after adjustment
recommended_action: Add cable-end length gauge and post-adjustment rotor-clearance check
critic_notes: Direct manual warning translated into DFMEA row; keep

function: Retain cable force path under braking load
potential_failure_mode: Cable anchor slips or cable strands are damaged at fixing bolt
potential_effect: Sudden severe reduction or loss of braking force
potential_cause: Insufficient cable fixing torque poor clamp geometry or strand cutting
current_control: Torque check and brake lever stroke check
recommended_action: Specify torque marking positive cable retention and strand-damage inspection

potential_failure_mode: Cable rust fraying stretch or housing drag reduces transmitted force
potential_effect: Progressive weak braking and increased stopping distance
potential_cause: Environmental corrosion abrasive routing or delayed cable replacement
current_control: Visual cable inspection and lever-stroke check
recommended_action: Define replacement interval routing bend limits and full-stroke return test

function: Convert cable pull into symmetric caliper actuation
potential_failure_mode: Actuator arm over-rotates and dislocates caliper parts
potential_effect: Lack of braking and loss of control
potential_cause: Mechanical stop weakness tolerance stack-up or overload at actuator arm
current_control: Functional brake test and actuator travel inspection
recommended_action: Add actuator travel stop verification and failure containment feature

function: Convert cable input into pad clamping force
potential_failure_mode: Mechanical disc brake caliper fails to clamp pads as commanded
potential_effect: Loss of braking power and crash risk
potential_cause: Internal caliper defect seized mechanism or failed linkage
current_control: Pre-ride brake function check and end-of-line test
recommended_action: Add caliper functional proof test supplier quality screen and recall traceability
critic_notes: General caliper failure row grounded in two recalls; keep

function: Maintain caliper position under vibration and braking load
potential_failure_mode: Caliper fixing bolt loosens or snap ring is omitted
potential_effect: Caliper shift rotor rub or severe braking loss
potential_cause: Insufficient torque missing locking device omitted snap ring or vibration loosening
current_control: Torque audit and visual snap-ring check
recommended_action: Use locking device torque stripe and mandatory snap-ring verification
critic_notes: Maps locking-device and snap-ring evidence; keep

function: Hold rotor centered in caliper slit
potential_failure_mode: Non-standard mounting boss or fork end causes rotor-caliper contact
potential_effect: Brake drag heat noise and reduced braking consistency
potential_cause: Mounting face dimensional error bracket misalignment or frame tolerance drift
recommended_action: Add mounting-face tolerance check and rotor-centering fixture
critic_notes: Interface-specific row distinct from bolt loosening; keep

function: Maintain equal pad-rotor clearance as pads wear
potential_failure_mode: Unequal or excessive pad clearance is left after wear adjustment
potential_effect: Insufficient braking force or unintended pad-rotor contact
potential_cause: Only one side adjusted cable barrel overused or pad screw ignored
recommended_action: Add two-sided clearance procedure with 0.2-0.5 mm target and sign-off

potential_failure_mode: Brake pad thickness falls below usable limit
potential_effect: Reduced braking margin and longer stopping distance
potential_cause: Normal wear missed inspection or delayed replacement
recommended_action: Add wear-limit gauge maintenance interval and user warning
critic_notes: Pad wear row is evidence-grounded and measurable; keep

function: Keep pad material and pad assembly retained in caliper
potential_failure_mode: Pad retention pin or split pin is omitted or not bent after replacement
potential_effect: Pad shift noise loss of contact area or inconsistent braking
potential_cause: Incorrect pad replacement procedure missing split pin or assembly oversight
recommended_action: Add pad-retention poka-yoke and second-person service check

potential_failure_mode: Oil or grease contaminates disc rotor or brake pads
potential_effect: Brake does not work correctly or braking becomes inconsistent
potential_cause: Lubricant overspray cleaning residue or handling contamination
current_control: Visual inspection and brake function check
recommended_action: Add contamination-control instruction replacement rule and clean handling zone

function: Provide stable friction surface and heat capacity
potential_failure_mode: Disc rotor cracks deforms or wears below serviceable thickness
potential_effect: Vibration reduced braking margin or sudden stop-use condition
potential_cause: Impact overheating abrasive wear or delayed inspection
recommended_action: Add rotor thickness gauge crack inspection and stop-use criterion
critic_notes: Combines rotor conditions stated in manual; keep

potential_failure_mode: Quick-release lever or adjacent hardware interferes with disc rotor
potential_effect: Mechanical interference drag or unsafe wheel-side condition
potential_cause: Lever positioned on rotor side or inadequate assembly clearance
recommended_action: Require rotor-side clearance check after wheel installation
critic_notes: Lower RPN but important assembly hazard; keep

function: Produce controllable deceleration under low-adhesion conditions
potential_failure_mode: Wet-road braking response is not adjusted by rider or system guidance
potential_effect: Longer braking distance skid and fall risk
potential_cause: Wet surface low tire friction and unchanged braking input
current_control: User manual warning and pre-ride judgement
recommended_action: Add wet-condition braking warning and conservative modulation guidance
critic_notes: Boundary note retained as wheel-level response not weather study; keep

potential_failure_mode: Front brake force rises too abruptly and locks the wheel
potential_effect: Forward fall and serious injury risk
potential_cause: Overpowered lever-caliper combination poor modulation or abrupt rider input
current_control: Brake feel check and compatibility review
recommended_action: Define modulation acceptance check and front-brake force progression target

function: Detect brake assembly defects before release or service return
potential_failure_mode: Final brake function and rotor-interference check is omitted
potential_effect: Latent cable pad or caliper defect reaches rider
potential_cause: Skipped service step time pressure or missing checklist
recommended_action: Add mandatory ten-actuation check wheel-rotation check and checklist signature
critic_notes: Process-control row tied to test and manual check evidence; keep
